description:
'''UK underground rap''' (often referred to as '''UK underground''' or '''UK UG''') is a [[hip-hop]] [[music]] scene and [[Musical style|style]] of [[UK rap]] that emerged in [[United Kingdom|the United Kingdom]] during the late [[2010s]] and early [[2020s (decade)|2020s]]. It has been described by [[Dazed|''Dazed'' magazine]] as "the UK's rap revolution".   

The scene differs from earlier waves of [[Underground hip-hop|underground]] [[UK rap|UK hip-hop]] through its strong influence from [[Music of the United States|American]] [[internet rap]] genres such as [[cloud rap]], [[Plugg music|plugg]], [[Rage music|rage]] and [[Jerk (music genre)|jerk]].   

Notable artists associated with the scene include [[EsDeeKid]], [[Fakemink]], Rico Ace, Fimiguerrero, [[Feng (rapper)|Feng]], [[Lancey Foux]], [[Jim Legxacy]], [[Niko B]] and [[YT (rapper)|YT]].  

Despite its original "underground" label, the scene has achieved significant critical and commercial success, and the term is now often used to refer to the genre stylistically rather than denoting popularity.
